title: Marketplace Plugin Manifest Contract
description: SSOT JSON-Schema (draft 2020-12) for the Molecule marketplace plugin manifest — the publishable shape of a `plugin.yaml` artifact (molecule-ai-plugin-* repos), authored marketplace-side per RFC molecule-core#3285 (Tool-Contract SSOT & Codegen). DERIVED FROM THE REAL ARTIFACTS + their CI validator: molecule-ai-plugin-molecule-careful-bash/plugin.yaml, image-gen/plugin.yaml, gh-identity/plugin.yaml, molecule-hitl/plugin.yaml, validated by each plugin repo's `.molecule-ci/scripts/validate-plugin.py` (required: name/version/description; runtimes must be a list; content is one of SKILL.md/hooks/skills/rules). The design is VS-Code-shaped: `engines` pins the minimum host version (like engines.vscode), and `contributes` is the OPEN, forward-compatible contribution surface — its KNOWN keys (skills/hooks/rules/mcpServers/commands, v1) are validated by shape while UNKNOWN contribution points (e.g. future themes/tabs/canvasElements) are tolerated (additionalProperties:true at the contributes level), so a newer plugin never fails this schema on an additive contribution point. The top-level `skills`/`hooks`/`rules` string lists mirror the real plugin.yaml v0 shorthand and remain accepted alongside the richer `contributes`. CANONICAL RUNTIMES: the `runtimes` enum is the SSOT reconciliation of the cross-artifact drift — the canonical form is the HYPHEN spelling (`claude-code`, matching the workspace/org templates and validate-workspace-template.py's known set); the legacy plugin UNDERSCORE spellings (`claude_code`, `gemini_cli`) used by today's plugin.yaml files are accepted as aliases (normalize to the hyphen form). The enum INCLUDES every runtime in use — claude-code, codex, hermes, openclaw, langgraph, autogen, crewai, deepagents, gemini-cli, google-adk, external — including deepagents/langgraph/autogen, which today's validate-plugin.py does not (it does not check the runtime enum) but real plugins already declare (gh-identity, molecule-hitl).

name.description: Plugin name (required by validate-plugin.py). The marketplace slug is derived from this. E.g. `molecule-careful-bash`, `image-gen`, `molecule-gh-identity`.

version.description: Plugin version (required). validate-plugin.py rejects any character outside `0-9.` — i.e. a dotted numeric version like `1.0.1` (no pre-release/build suffix). Modelled here with a numeric-dotted pattern mirroring that check.

publisher.description: Optional marketplace publisher/namespace (VS-Code `publisher`). Distinct from `author` (free-text attribution). Falls back to the org when absent.

author.description: Optional free-text author attribution as it appears in plugin.yaml (e.g. `Molecule AI`).

kind.description: Optional v1 plugin kind discriminator from plugin.yaml (e.g. `env-mutator` for the Go env-mutator class — gh-identity). Absent for skill/hook-class plugins. Open string (not enum) so new kinds are additive.

entrypoint.description: Optional Go entrypoint for code-class plugins (e.g. `pluginloader.BuildRegistry` — gh-identity). Absent for content-only plugins.

engines.description: Host-version constraints (VS-Code `engines`). Pins the minimum Molecule host the plugin requires.

runtimes.description: Runtimes this plugin supports (plugin.yaml `runtimes`; validate-plugin.py requires a list). Items use the CANONICAL runtime enum (see schema description): hyphen form canonical, underscore form accepted as alias.

skills.description: v0 shorthand: bare skill names contributed by the plugin (plugin.yaml `skills: [careful-mode]`). The richer structured form is `contributes.skills`.

hooks.description: v0 shorthand: bare hook names contributed by the plugin (plugin.yaml `hooks: [pre-bash-careful]`). The richer structured form is `contributes.hooks`.

rules.description: v0 shorthand: bare rule names contributed by the plugin. The richer structured form is `contributes.rules`.

contributes.description: VS-Code-shaped contribution surface. OPEN object: known contribution points are validated by shape; unknown ones are tolerated (forward-compat).

privileged.description: Whether the plugin requires org-admin / management privilege (e.g. the `molecule-platform` management MCP). image-gen explicitly documents `NOT privileged`. Absent/false = normal user capability.

sha256.description: Optional content digest of the published plugin artifact, for the marketplace integrity/attestation layer (Phase 3). Hex sha256.

runtimeId.description: CANONICAL runtime identifier. Hyphen form is canonical (`claude-code`); underscore forms (`claude_code`, `gemini_cli`) are accepted legacy aliases that normalize to the hyphen form. The enum is the union of the canonical set and those two aliases, and INCLUDES deepagents/langgraph/autogen (which real plugins declare).

engines.description: Host engine constraints, like VS-Code `engines`. `molecule` pins the minimum host version as a semver range. Open object so future host engines (e.g. a runtime-specific engine) are additive.

molecule.description: Minimum Molecule host version as a semver range, e.g. `^1.0`.

contributes.description: The contribution surface. KNOWN keys (v1) below are validated by shape. UNKNOWN keys (future contribution points such as `themes`, `tabs`, `canvasElements`) are explicitly TOLERATED via additionalProperties:true — adding a new contribution point MUST NOT fail validation against this schema (forward-compat).

hooks.description: Structured hook contributions (e.g. a PreToolUse:Bash hook).

mcpServers.description: MCP server contributions. An mcpServers-shaped plugin is resolved to the MCP server adaptor for any runtime (see mcp/mcp-plugin-delivery.contract.json). The entry shape mirrors the runtime-agnostic descriptor name->{command,args?,env?}.

path.description: Optional path to the skill content within the plugin.

event.description: Optional host event the hook binds to (e.g. `PreToolUse:Bash`).

mcpServerContribution.description: A single contributed MCP server descriptor (runtime-agnostic name->{command,args?,env?}).

env.description: Optional environment map (var->value) for the MCP server process.

privileged.description: Whether this MCP server is the privileged management surface.
